key_insight: Dynamic LLM-generated reward weights can break stationarity in off-policy cooperative MARL, so stabilizing the shaping signal is crucial and its benefit depends on the baseline regime.
engineering_relevance: For practical MARL systems, reward shaping should be treated as a time-consistent control signal, not a continuously drifting prompt output, especially when using replay buffers.
method: Phase-Based Freeze Schedule: keep LLM reward weights fixed within each training phase.
Exponential Moving Average (EMA): smooth per-episode weight updates to limit drift.
Tested with QMIX on three cooperative environments, plus an exploratory VDN extension.
evidence_signal: Across five seeds and three environments, EMA improved Simple Spread success from 74.4% to 86.7% while naive dynamic updates fell to 15.2%; in Level-Based Foraging, EMA enabled 95.9% success from a 0.1% baseline.
watch_item: Because replay buffers store rewards under old shaping weights, any online update scheme must preserve reward-label consistency or it can destabilize learning.
paper_title: Regime-Conditional Stabilisation of LLM-Augmented Cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
